title: Crisp Caramelized Asian Wood Ear Mushroom Skewers
description: A malty, glassy-jelly, show-off polish dish featuring wood ear mushrooms, robata-grilled to perfection and served on a hot stone plate, this recipe is an ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly delight.
instructions: Soak the dried wood ear mushrooms in hot water for 30 minutes, then drain and slice them into thin strips.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey, garlic, and ginger to make the marinade.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the marinade and toss to coat, letting them marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat, threading the marinated mushrooms onto skewers.
Brush the grill with vegetable oil to prevent sticking, then grill the mushroom skewers for 3-4 minutes per side, or until caramelized and crispy.
Brush the skewers with sesame oil and season with salt and black pepper during the last minute of grilling.
Serve the skewers on a hot stone plate, garnished with sliced green onions and a side of steamed vegetables if desired.
Let the skewers rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
